title: [phase:plan] Review the task-completion backup auto-record path. Focus on root cause for a background scan process hanging ~34 minutes and blocking explicit record dispatch via scan lock. Inspect only these files: /Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/task_backup.py, /U...
summary_text: 1. Most likely root cause: `scan_codex_task_completions()` does expensive work per JSONL line, not per completion event. It calls `_codex_audit_snapshot()` (session-wide audit) and `_recent_active_models()` (full active-model log scan) inside the inner record loop before filtering to `task_complete`, so runtime can balloon to tens of minutes. During that time, `run-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh` holds `task-completion-backup.scan.lock`, and explicit record mode detects that lock and forces `--no-gha`, blocking immediate dispatch. 2. Minimal safe code change: in `task_backup.py`, in `scan_codex_task_completions()`, fast-filter to `task_complete` first, then run expensive enrichment only for those records; cache `_codex_audit_snapshot()` once per session file (lazy-initialize on first matching event). Leave lock policy unchanged. 3. Biggest regression risk: filter/enrichment logic divergence from `_build_codex_completion_record()` could accidentally skip legitimate completion events (false negatives). The guard is to centralize the predicate/helper so scan and record-building criteria cannot drift.
